probes.language: Passage and all 20 options are English; spelling variety consistently BrE (traveller, ageing); zero AmE leaks. language=CLEAR upheld.
probes.verbatim_quotes: All five rationale quotes match the current passage text verbatim (modulo gap markers): 'quietly ___ its overnight services', 'a loss ... willing to ___ indefinitely', 'expected to fill perhaps half the cabins' + 'sold out weeks in advance', 'more ___ than the cheering headlines suggest', 'the ledgers look far less ___'. No stale quotes.
probes.collocation_factcheck: Every rationale collocation claim verified: (1) 'withdrew its services' idiomatic; dismissed/declined/renounced fail on collocation/sense; the intransitive-reduce-sense note on 'decline' is linguistically correct. (2) 'underwrite a loss' idiomatic; condone requires wrongdoing, overturn takes decision/verdict, dispel takes doubts/fears/myths. (3) 'Instead' carries counter-to-expectation reversal; Accordingly/Otherwise/Meanwhile mis-signal. (4) 'precarious' is the negative pole demanded by 'Yet' + downstream fragility evidence; auspicious is the polarity mirror, gregarious/meticulous sense-misfit. (5) 'less rosy' collocates with a favourable financial picture; hollow/candid/generous fail.
probes.key_sanity: All five keys are the unique idiomatic best answer and map to the correct option text (1=C withdrew, 2=A underwrite, 3=D Instead, 4=B precarious, 5=C rosy). No distractor is defensible as a second key. POS uniformity holds per gap (past-tense transitive verbs; base-form verbs after 'willing to'; sentence adverbs; -ious adjectives; adjectives).
probes.key_spread: C/A/D/B/C matches schema_note; no problematic position clustering (no adjacent repeats, no letter used more than twice).
probes.gap_integrity: All five ___(n)___ markers present in passage; questions[] q_index/prompt align 1:1 with gaps.
